releaseNotes:
- Stops new batch wallets from receiving a free starter coin.
- Reverses the old batch starter coin once when an existing batch wallet loads.
- Prevents batch wallets from creating free coins by applying referral codes; verified normal student referrals still count for rewards.
- Keeps batch wallets fundable through gifts and real referral rewards.

history.releaseNotes:
- Stops new batch wallets from receiving a free starter coin.
- Reverses the old batch starter coin once when an existing batch wallet loads.
- Prevents batch wallets from creating free coins by applying referral codes; verified normal student referrals still count for rewards.
- Keeps batch wallets fundable through gifts and real referral rewards.

- Adds a GitHub API fallback so the updater can read fresh release notes even when raw files are cached.
- Keeps raw GitHub and GitHub Pages as fallback update channels.
- Keeps cached release notes available during temporary network problems.

- Hardens the update system with built-in GitHub fallback URLs even if a build flag is missing.
- Caches the last good update manifest so release notes can still open during temporary network issues.
- Keeps the Settings update card usable instead of failing when release notes temporarily cannot refresh.

- Fixes the release notes card so it can read the GitHub update channel again.
- Keeps batch wallet referral rewards and Apply referral code available.
- Keeps the How to get coins guide instead of direct developer coin purchases.

- Adds referral rewards and Apply referral code to batch wallets.
- Replaces the demo purchase card with a How to get coins guide.
- Explains safe coin options: trusted friend transfers or 3 successful referrals for 1 coin.

- Hides raw server, Firebase, platform, and network error details from normal users.
- Shows safe public messages unless BELSEH_DEBUG_ERRORS is explicitly enabled for a diagnostic build.
- Stops native Android download/share errors and debug logs from exposing technical details.

- Extends the app background and overlays behind the Android status bar at the top of the phone.
- Uses transparent edge-to-edge system bars so the time area matches the current screen theme.
- Keeps status and navigation icon contrast correct in light and dark mode.

- Fixes the update popups so the card surface is solid and no screen text shows through.
- Keeps the premium blue update dialog style in light and dark mode.
- Makes the release-note panel opaque while preserving the soft tinted design.

- Shows only the latest 10 update-history entries first, with Load more for older releases.
- Removes ledger entry and transfer ID from transaction details.
- Makes the transaction detail header inherit the same color treatment as the transaction card.

- Restyles update popups to match the app's premium blue theme in light and dark mode.
- Uses the same release-note list, status pills, and action button style as Settings.
- Makes update checks tolerate hosted JSON encoding marks safely.

- Restyles update popups to match the app's premium blue theme in light and dark mode.
- Uses the same release-note list style and action buttons as Settings.
- Keeps update install and installed-summary behavior unchanged.

- Adds Which account should I choose? on the sign-in type screen.
- Adds a guide explaining Normal account vs Batch account and which wallet pays.
- Keeps batch wallet sign-in usable even if cloud student-account restore is blocked by Firestore rules.

- Makes the large Settings update-card icon a real refresh button with loading feedback.
- Adds View update history so users can read text from previous releases.
- Keeps update notes as clear list items.

- Combines Settings What's new and Check update into one larger update card.
- Keeps release notes, update status, refresh, and install/check action in the same place.
- Reduces Settings clutter without making the update card compact.

- Fixes batch wallet login permission timing so Firestore waits for the Google batch account before reading or writing wallet data.
- Stops coin gifts to unknown student Form Four indexes; the recipient must already have logged in to Belseh once.
- Keeps What's new release notes as clear list items instead of one long story.

- Adds a What's new card on Settings so users can revisit the latest release notes at any time.
- Shows update status and refreshes release notes.
- Opens the updater from the same place.

- Fixes saved student account avatars.
- Keeps real profile photos on normal and batch account lists.
- Handles real accounts whose name matched the old placeholder fallback.

- Refines the launch splash screen.
- Keeps the Belseh logo compact across phone sizes.
- Improves consistency across Android versions.

- Fixes student profile loading when DIDIS profile data is slow or missing.
- Prevents another student's saved name from appearing on a different account.
- Uses neutral account-specific fallback text until the real profile loads.

- Publishes a universal APK.
- Adds arm64-v8a, armeabi-v7a, and x86_64 support.
- Fixes compatibility on more Android phones, including 64-bit-only devices.

- Improves app smoothness with consistent page transitions.
- Preserves tab scroll position.
- Improves scroll caching and list building on history and transaction screens.

- Cleans up transaction detail ledger display.
- Shows ledger IDs as compact entries.
- Adds a Copy full ID action instead of showing a long raw string.

- Adds one launch popup after an update is installed.
- Explains what changed in the installed version.
- Shows the installed-update popup only once for that version.
- Shows the launch update popup only when a newer APK is available to install.

- Adds a premium startup update window when a newer APK exists.
- Shows what is new before install.
- Shows installed and latest published version when the app is current.

- Fixes switch profile on the History screen.
- Tapping the top profile control now returns to account selection immediately.

- Updates dark theme top cards on Home and Account.
- Uses deeper blue so first cards match the night palette.

- Adds one-tap APK sharing from Settings.
- Supports local phone-to-phone installs.
- Refreshes Home, Account, Settings, and Transaction cards with a softer blue premium style.

- Makes update downloads persistent.
- Shows Android notification progress.
- Allows cancellation from the app or notification panel.

- Sets coin price to TSH 500 by default.
- Changes referrals so both accounts receive 1 coin.

- Refreshes the update page.
- Adds a premium status hero and release card.
- Improves loading and error states.
- Makes Check update and Quick note large premium cards in Settings.

- Adds the GitHub update channel.
- Adds the Settings update entry.
